og.disciplinedLine: Disciplined craft — technical accountability. Calibrate stroke, voltage, and style with DialedIn.

howItWorks.title: The Science of the Sweet Spot
howItWorks.description: Inside the DialedIn logic: Understanding the 3.5mm pivot, adaptive voltage logic, and style-first technical baselines.

science.title: Scientific methodology
science.description: Universal machine physics for tattoo setup: hammer effect on long stroke, ACUS Hz readouts, CPS consistency across hardware tiers, and membrane-aware voltage compensation for stiffer cartridges.

blogHub.title: Technical Blog
blogHub.description: Stroke physics, needle geometry, hardware tiers, and disciplined setup methodology from DialedIn.

blogStrokePhysics.title: Stroke Physics: The 3.5mm Pivot Guide
blogStrokePhysics.description: Why your tattoo machine's stroke dictates your voltage. Mastering the relationship between mechanical force and needle frequency.

blogNeedleGeometry.title: Needle Geometry: #10 vs #12 & Taper
blogNeedleGeometry.description: Understanding how needle gauge and taper length affect ink flow and skin trauma. Technical standards for professional tattooing.

blogCartridgeDrag.title: Mastering Cartridge Drag & Skin Tension
blogCartridgeDrag.description: Overcoming membrane resistance (The Membrane Tax) and using three-point stretch techniques to ensure pigment saturation.

blogHandSpeedSync.title: Syncing Hand Speed to Machine Hertz
blogHandSpeedSync.description: Technical guide on coordinating hand velocity with machine frequency (CPS) for perfect stitch density and texture.

blogHardwareTiers.title: Tattoo Hardware: Tier 1 vs Tier 2 Gear
blogHardwareTiers.description: A practical comparison of benchmark brands vs workhorse gear. When to upgrade your machine for professional consistency.

blogNeedleHangDepth.title: Mastering Needle Hang & Depth Control
blogNeedleHangDepth.description: The secret to visibility and precision. Why riding the tube vs. hanging the needle changes your saturation and safety.

blogHertzVsVolts.title: Hertz vs. Volts: The Frequency Truth
blogHertzVsVolts.description: Tattoo machine Hertz-to-Volts explained for ACUS M1, Cheyenne, and standard rotary setups, with CPS/hand-speed sync guidance.

blogMembraneTax.title: The Membrane Tax: Why Your Machine Stalls on Budget Cartridges
blogMembraneTax.description: Tattoo machine stalling explained: membrane tension, rotary torque loss, and practical voltage compensation for Dragonhawk Mast and other tier-2 setups.

dialedInUi.toolGuardrail: Technical suggestion only — final machine adjustment is the artist’s responsibility.

common.operatorNote: DialedIn.ink is operated by Albor Digital LLC. These pages are the public summary aligned with Albor Digital legal documentation.

disclaimer.title: Technical Disclaimer
disclaimer.lead: DialedIn is an educational and decision-support tool intended solely for informational purposes. Suggested parameters (voltage, hertz, stroke, and needle configuration) reflect general industry consensus and manufacturer specifications; they do not replace professional judgment or training under a qualified mentor.
disclaimer.acknowledge: By using this tool, you acknowledge that:
disclaimer.riskBody: Tattooing involves invasive procedures. Incorrect machine use or inappropriate settings can cause severe skin trauma or infections.
disclaimer.equipBody: Machines and cartridges differ by brand and maintenance condition; DialedIn does not guarantee exact outcomes.
disclaimer.liabilityBody: Albor Digital LLC is not liable for injuries, legal claims, or unsatisfactory aesthetic results. Final machine adjustment remains solely the artist’s responsibility.

privacy.title: Privacy & Data Transparency
privacy.lead: At DialedIn, we value your craft and your privacy.
privacy.dataBody: We do not request sensitive personal information. We only store configuration preferences anonymously to improve our logic engine.
privacy.aiBody: This tool uses language models to process technical taxonomies. We do not process personal data through these models.
privacy.cookiesBody: We use essential cookies to remember your language and equipment preferences for the session.

terms.title: Guild Rules (Terms of Service)
terms.useBody: This tool is for individual use by apprentices and tattoo professionals. Bulk extraction (scraping) of our technical logic is prohibited.
terms.ipBody: The relational logic and interface design are the exclusive property of Albor Digital LLC.
terms.lawBody: Any legal dispute shall be governed by the laws of the State of Wyoming, United States.

responsibleAi.title: Responsible AI Policy
responsibleAi.lead: DialedIn.ink is operated by Albor Digital LLC. This policy describes how we use automation and language models transparently.
responsibleAi.purposeBody: DialedIn combines deterministic rules and documented heuristics with optional language-model assistance for technical taxonomy labeling. It is not a medical device and must not replace professional judgment or manufacturer documentation.
responsibleAi.humanBody: Artists must validate every recommendation against manufacturer specs, skin type, session goals, and local regulations. The tool surfaces warnings where modeled checks flag potential mismatch.
responsibleAi.dataBody: We do not send identifiable personal data through language models for profiling. Taxonomy and preference handling follow this Privacy summary and essential cookie practices.
responsibleAi.accuracyBody: We version logic and publish a visible last-update stamp on the site. Material model changes will be reflected in release notes or methodology pages where practical.
